summary: A solid West Coast show with a standout Victim and an inspired second‑set UJB/Dark Star jam, captured on a clean soundboard recording.
review: The concert is praised for its strong second set – especially the powerful Victim performance and an extended Uncle John’s Band into a purposeful Dark Star. Reviewers note Brent Mydland’s organ presence and overall good band chemistry, while the soundboard source delivers clear separation with no major audio flaws.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the show positively (4‑star comments) and mention good sound quality; a few note rhythm issues and drum tempo problems, yielding an overall strong but not flawless rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No significant complaints; reviewers comment on excellent separation and clean capture, indicating a clear soundboard source.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is described as a bit ragged with some new‑tune hiccups, but the second set shifts mood dramatically – Victim opens a powerful stretch that flows into an extended Uncle John’s Band/Dark Star sequence praised for its purpose and intensity.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Backed up Bob on vocals and added strong backing vocal harmonies; his tone is noted as solid throughout.
band_member_comments.Bob: His lead vocals are supported by Jerry’s backup; no specific criticism.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland's organ work is highlighted repeatedly – described as a major influence and “outstanding” on the recording.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mixed feedback: some feel Bill or Mickey struggled to lock in on Victim, suggesting tempo issues.
